prompt: Write a simple 4-bar hook with a repeating motif — something catchy at 120 BPM.

args.content:
ch 1 Melody
  plugin mini
  strip console1

ch 2 Bass
  plugin bass
  strip console1

ch 3 Chords
  plugin jup8
  strip console1

ch 4 Kick
  plugin kick

ch 5 Snare
  plugin drums

ch 6 HiHat
  plugin drums

arr hook 4 verse 4 chorus 4

sec hook
  ch 1 Melody
    p x x x x  x x x x  x x x x  x x x x
    n 72 72 71 72  _ 76 74 72  74 74 72 74  _ 77 76 74
    v 100 80 90 100 0 110 90 80 100 80 90 100 0 110 90 80

  ch 2 Bass
    p q x x q q
    n 36 36 36 36 36

  ch 3 Chords
    p w
    n 60 64 67

  ch 4 Kick
    p q q q q
    n 36

  ch 5 Snare
    p _q q _q q
    n 38

  ch 6 HiHat
    p x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x x
    n 42

sec verse
  ch 1 Melody
    p q q q q
    n 60 62 64 65

  ch 2 Bass
    p q _q q _q
    n 36

  ch 3 Chords
    p w
    n 48 52 55

  ch 4 Kick
    p q _q q _q
    n 36

  ch 5 Snare
    p _q q _q q
    n 38

  ch 6 HiHat
    p x x x x x x x x
    n 42

sec chorus
  ch 1 Melody
    p q q q q
    n 65 67 69 72

  ch 2 Bass
    p q q _q q
    n 36

  ch 3 Chords
    p w
    n 53 57 60

  ch 4 Kick
    p q _q q _q
    n 36

  ch 5 Snare
    p _q q _q q
    n 38

  ch 6 HiHat
    p x x x x x x x x
    n 42

judgment.relevance_reason: The AI set the BPM to 120 and created a repeating motif, but added unrequested verse and chorus sections and failed to use proper BIRD syntax for rests.
judgment.quality_reason: The motif relies on an invalid syntax (placing '_' in the note array instead of the rhythm pattern), though the implicit 1-bar looping achieves the requested repetition.
